audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim wilderness-memory, manna, good-land, and wealth-warning discourse, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for wilderness humbling/testing, manna, not-by-bread-alone, parental discipline imagery, good-land catalogue, satisfaction and blessing, wealth/forgetting warning, and power-to-gain-wealth theology.

verses.restored: All command I command you today you shall keep to do, so you may live and multiply and enter and possess land YHWH swore to your fathers.
You shall remember all way YHWH your Divine led you these forty years in wilderness, to humble you, test you, and know what was in your heart, whether you would keep his commands or not.

He humbled you and made you hunger and fed you manna, which you did not know and your fathers did not know, to make you know that not by bread alone does humanity live, but by everything going out from mouth of YHWH does humanity live.
Your garment did not wear out upon you, and your foot did not swell these forty years.
Know with your heart that as a man disciplines/instructs his son, YHWH your Divine disciplines/instructs you.
You shall keep commands of YHWH your Divine, to walk in his ways and fear/revere him.
For YHWH your Divine is bringing you into good land, land of streams of water, springs and deep waters going out in valley and mountain,
land of wheat and barley, vine and fig and pomegranate, land of olive oil and honey/date syrup,
land where you shall eat bread without scarcity, you shall lack nothing in it, land whose stones are iron and from whose mountains you may quarry copper.
You shall eat and be satisfied, and bless YHWH your Divine for good land he gave you.
Guard yourself lest you forget YHWH your Divine by not keeping his commands and judgments and statutes that I command you today,
lest you eat and are satisfied and build good houses and dwell in them,
and your cattle and flocks multiply, silver and gold multiply for you, and all you have multiplies,
and your heart is lifted up and you forget YHWH your Divine who brought you out from land of Egypt, from house of slaves,
who led you through great and fearful wilderness, with fiery serpent and scorpion and thirsty ground where there was no water, who brought water for you from flint rock,
who fed you manna in wilderness that your fathers did not know, to humble you and test you, to do good to you in your latter end,
and you say in your heart, My power and might of my hand made me this wealth.
You shall remember YHWH your Divine, for he is the one giving you power to make/gain wealth, in order to establish his covenant he swore to your fathers, as this day.
It shall be, if you surely forget YHWH your Divine and go after other gods and serve them and bow to them, I testify against you today that you shall surely perish.
Like nations YHWH causes to perish from before you, so you shall perish, because you did not listen to voice of YHWH your Divine.

translation_notes: The wilderness is interpreted as humbling and testing, but this must not be generalized into a claim that every deprivation, abuse, or disaster is divinely imposed for character development.
'Not by bread alone' does not minimize material hunger; the same verse first says YHWH fed the people manna.
Parental-discipline imagery must never justify physical abuse or coercive religious discipline.
The good-land catalogue celebrates water, biodiversity, staple crops, fruit, minerals, satisfaction, and gratitude.
The central economic warning is against self-made mythology: wealth can lift the heart into forgetting slavery, dependence, community, and Divine gift.
Power to gain wealth is covenant responsibility, not prosperity-gospel proof of personal virtue.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 8 to romanticize hunger, excuse abuse, shame poverty, preach prosperity theology, or claim wealth proves Divine favor. Its enduring warning is that abundance becomes dangerous when people forget liberation, dependence, gratitude, and the labor supporting their comfort.
